result.signature:
pub fn fold_many0<I, E, F, G, H, R>(
  parser: F,
  init: H,
  g: G,
) -> impl Parser<I, Output = R, Error = E>
where
  I: Clone + InputLength,
  F: Parser<I, Error = E>,
  G: FnMut(R, <F as Parser<I>>::Output) -> R,
  H: FnMut() -> R,
  E: ParseError<I>,
{
  FoldMany0 {
    parser,
    g,
    init,
    r: PhantomData,
  }
}

length_data
result.signature:
pub fn length_data<I, E, F>(f: F) -> impl Parser<I, Output = I, Error = E>
where
  I: Input,
  <F as Parser<I>>::Output: ToUsize,
  F: Parser<I, Error = E>,
  E: ParseError<I>,
{
  f.flat_map(|size| take(size))
}

result.signature:
pub fn length_count<I, E, F, G>(
  f: F,
  g: G,
) -> impl Parser<I, Output = Vec<<G as Parser<I>>::Output>, Error = E>
where
  I: Clone,
  <F as Parser<I>>::Output: ToUsize,
  F: Parser<I, Error = E>,
  G: Parser<I, Error = E>,
  E: ParseError<I>,
{
  LengthCount {
    length: f,
    parser: g,
    e: PhantomData,
  }
}

result.signature:
pub fn many<I, E, Collection, F, G>(
  range: G,
  parser: F,
) -> impl Parser<I, Output = Collection, Error = E>
where
  I: Clone + InputLength,
  F: Parser<I, Error = E>,
  Collection: Extend<<F as Parser<I>>::Output> + Default,
  E: ParseError<I>,
  G: NomRange<usize>,
{
  Many {
    parser,
    range,
    c: PhantomData,
  }
}

pub fn fold<I, E, F, G, H, J, R>(
  range: J,
  parser: F,
  init: H,

) -> impl Parser<I, Output = R, Error = E>
where
  I: Clone + InputLength,

  G: FnMut(R, <F as Parser<I>>::Output) -> R,
  H: FnMut() -> R,
  E: ParseError<I>,
  J: NomRange<usize>,
{
  Fold {
    parser,
    init,
    fold,
    range,
  }
}

result.name: take_s_succeed
many1_count

parts.text: The function `many1_count` is designed to repeatedly apply a given parser to an input until the parser fails, counting the number of successful applications. It requires at least one successful parse to avoid returning an error. It takes a parser function as input and returns the count of successful parses as an unsigned integer, or an error if the first application of the parser fails.
